proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_030 carries no information about objective_response because it is a constant column.
Some of the binary features in this prostate cohort are randomized treatment indicators with main effects on objective_response, while others are disease/biomarker features.

analyses.code: df['feature_030'].nunique()  # = 1, range [0,0]
analyses.result_summary: feature_030 has a single unique value (0) for all 50000 patients and is uninformative. It is dropped from subsequent analyses.

analyses.code: for each binary feature b: chi2_contingency(crosstab(b, objective_response)) and crosstab(b, every other feature) to gauge correlation profile.
analyses.result_summary: Of the 12 binary features, all 12 are essentially uncorrelated with the other features (no chi-square or t-test p<0.001 except feature_013 vs feature_022). Marginal response-rate differences flag feature_008 (+0.202, p~0), feature_013 (-0.193, p~0), feature_015 (-0.101, p~1e-98), feature_021 (-0.101, p~5e-56), and feature_027 (-0.066, p~3e-9) as candidate effect-bearing features. feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_023, feature_004 show |delta|<0.005 (p>0.3) -- candidate randomized treatments with possible HTE only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 has a strong positive main effect on objective_response (higher response rate when feature_008=1 than when feature_008=0).
feature_013 has a strong negative main effect on objective_response (lower response rate when feature_013=1 than when feature_013=0).

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_008); chi2 on 2x2
analyses.result_summary: feature_008=1 vs 0: response 0.361 vs 0.159, delta = +0.202 absolute (OR = 2.99, chi-square p ~ 0). Strongly supports h3.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_013); chi2 on 2x2
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 vs 0: response 0.154 vs 0.346, delta = -0.193 absolute (OR = 0.343, chi-square p ~ 0). Strongly supports h4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 (continuous, range 0.12-3622) is negatively associated with objective_response: higher feature_022 predicts lower response.
feature_001 (ordinal 0/1/2) is negatively associated with objective_response: higher level predicts lower response.
feature_021, feature_015, and feature_027 (binary) each independently lower objective_response when present.

analyses.code: pointbiserialr(objective_response, feature_022); mean diff
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_022 = 28.2 in responders vs 47.2 in non-responders (point-biserial r = -0.087, p ~ 3e-85). Strongly supports h5.

analyses.code: groupby(feature_001).objective_response.mean()
analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_001 level: 0->0.282, 1->0.228, 2->0.182. Monotone decreasing. point-biserial r = -0.081, p ~ 2e-73. Supports h6.

analyses.code: for f in [feature_021, feature_015, feature_027]: chi2_contingency(crosstab(f, objective_response))
analyses.result_summary: feature_021=1 vs 0: 0.150 vs 0.250 (OR 0.527, p ~ 5e-56). feature_015=1 vs 0: 0.160 vs 0.260 (OR 0.541, p ~ 2e-98). feature_027=1 vs 0: 0.176 vs 0.242 (OR 0.668, p ~ 3e-9). All three significantly lower response. Supports h7.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic regression, feature_008 remains a strong independent positive predictor of objective_response and feature_013 a strong independent negative predictor.

Many binary features (feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_023, feature_004) are NOT main-effect predictors of objective_response (they may be randomized treatments with treatment-effect heterogeneity only, or noise variables).

Continuous features feature_016 (likely age 30-90), feature_010 (Gleason-like 6-10), feature_018, feature_026, feature_007, feature_032, feature_014 have no detectable main effect on objective_response in this cohort.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(objective_response, sm.add_constant(df[all_features_except_030])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Independent significant predictors (multivariable logistic): feature_008 (beta=+1.20, p~0), feature_013 (beta=-1.13, p~0), feature_015 (beta=-0.69, p~0), feature_021 (beta=-0.74, p~0), feature_027 (beta=-0.43, p~3e-9), feature_001 (beta=-0.33, p~0), feature_022 (beta=-0.001, p~3e-8), feature_020 (beta=-0.036, p~2e-33), feature_024 (beta=-0.007, p~9e-8), feature_002 (beta=+0.127, p~2e-8). Pseudo-R2 = 0.124. Supports h8 with feature_008 as the strongest positive driver.

analyses.code: univariate chi2 for feature_005, 006, 011, 017, 019, 023, 004
analyses.result_summary: All seven binaries have |delta_response| < 0.005 with p > 0.3 (feature_005 p=0.305, feature_006 p=0.871, feature_011 p=0.914, feature_017 p=0.886, feature_019 p=0.600, feature_023 p=0.470, feature_004 p=0.876). Consistent with no main effect; supports h9.

analyses.code: pointbiserialr for each continuous feature

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 is essentially a dichotomization of the continuous feature_022 (high feature_022 -> feature_013=1) and the two encode the same underlying disease state.

analyses.code: groupby(qcut(feature_022, 4)).feature_013.mean(); ks_2samp(feature_022 by feature_013); describe(feature_022 | feature_013)
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_013 by feature_022 quartile: Q1=0.19, Q2=0.41, Q3=0.67, Q4=0.93 (monotone). Median feature_022 = 7.4 when feature_013=0 vs 32.9 when feature_013=1. KS statistic = 0.51, p~0. Strongly supports h11: feature_013 is approximately a high-vs-low feature_022 indicator -- they encode the same disease/biomarker construct.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 is a treatment indicator. Its positive effect on objective_response is largest in patients with feature_013=0 and is essentially abolished in patients with feature_013=1.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 * feature_013); stratified response rates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -1.978, p ~ 0. Stratified response rates: feature_013=0: t=1 -> 0.610, t=0 -> 0.169 (delta +0.441, OR=7.7). feature_013=1: t=1 -> 0.158, t=0 -> 0.150 (delta +0.008, OR=1.06, p=0.084). Profound treatment-effect heterogeneity: feature_008 only works in feature_013=0 patients. Strongly supports h12.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_022 modifies the feature_008 treatment effect: the treatment benefit decreases monotonically as feature_022 rises.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 * z(feature_022)); stratified by feature_022 quartile
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta on standardized feature_022 = -0.81, p ~ 1e-72. Treatment effect by quartile: Q1 (0.12-6.46) +0.367; Q2 (6.5-15.9) +0.260; Q3 (15.9-41.3) +0.148; Q4 (41.3-3622) +0.034. Monotone attenuation. Strongly supports h13 -- consistent with h11 that feature_022 and feature_013 capture the same axis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 is a second effect modifier that suppresses the feature_008 treatment effect: when feature_015=1, the feature_008 effect is essentially zero, even among feature_013=0 patients.
feature_021 is a third effect modifier that reduces or abolishes the feature_008 treatment effect when feature_021=1.
feature_027 is a fourth effect modifier that reduces the feature_008 treatment effect when feature_027=1.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 * feature_015); 4-way crosstab feature_008 x feature_013 x feature_015
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -1.237, p ~ 1.4e-93. In feature_013=0 patients: feature_015=0 t=1->0.717 vs t=0->0.171 (delta +0.546); feature_015=1 t=1->0.177 vs t=0->0.163 (delta +0.014). feature_015=1 abolishes treatment effect even when feature_013=0. Supports h14.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 * feature_021)
logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 * feature_027)

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-level ordinal) modifies the feature_008 treatment effect: at higher levels of feature_001, the absolute treatment benefit is somewhat smaller (because baseline response is lower).

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 * feature_001); response by feature_001 level x feature_008
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.096 on the logit scale, p ~ 1.6e-5. On the absolute scale, treatment effect is +0.207 (level 0), +0.201 (level 1), +0.198 (level 2) -- nearly constant in absolute terms but smaller in relative-risk terms at higher levels. Marginally supports h17 but the absolute heterogeneity is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary features without main effect (feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_023, feature_004) NONE shows a clinically meaningful treatment-effect heterogeneity pattern when used as a candidate treatment with respect to other features.

analyses.code: for each candidate tx in [005,006,011,017,019,023,004]: logit(objective_response ~ tx * f) for every other feature f; rank by interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Best interaction p-values per candidate: feature_005 p=0.047 (feature_027); feature_006 p=0.010 (feature_019); feature_011 p=0.023 (feature_007); feature_017 p=0.031 (feature_028); feature_019 p=0.010 (feature_006); feature_023 p=0.031 (feature_021); feature_004 p=0.033 (feature_010). After multiple-testing across ~30 features per treatment, none survive a Bonferroni-adjusted threshold (~0.0017). No reproducible HTE signal. Supports h18: these features behave like noise / non-effect-modifying covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint subgroup defined by feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0, feature_008 produces an extremely large treatment effect on objective_response: response rises from ~17% (feature_008=0) to ~80% (feature_008=1), with OR > 15.

analyses.code: fav = (feature_013==0)&(feature_015==0)&(feature_021==0)&(feature_027==0); subset response by feature_008; logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 | fav)
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup N=15681 (31% of cohort). Response rate: feature_008=1 -> 0.798, feature_008=0 -> 0.172, absolute delta = +0.626. Logistic OR = 18.98 (p ~ 0). Strongly supports h19.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_001=0 to the favorable subgroup further enriches for treatment effect of feature_008 (because lower feature_001 means higher baseline response).

Restricting to feature_022 below its median (in addition to all four favorable binary markers) does not further increase the absolute treatment effect of feature_008, because feature_013=0 already implies low feature_022.

analyses.code: fav2 = fav & (feature_001==0); subset response by feature_008
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup N=5511. Response: feature_008=1 -> 0.859, feature_008=0 -> 0.213, delta = +0.646. OR = 22.43 (p ~ 0). Slight further enrichment vs h19 (delta 0.626 -> 0.646). Supports h20.

analyses.code: fav3 = fav2 & (feature_022 <= median); response by feature_008
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup N=4314. Response: feature_008=1 -> 0.863, feature_008=0 -> 0.223, delta = +0.640. Essentially the same as fav2 (within noise). Confirms h21: feature_022 adds little once feature_013=0 is imposed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the unfavorable mirror subgroup (feature_013=1 OR feature_015=1 OR feature_021=1 OR feature_027=1), feature_008 has essentially zero treatment effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: unfav = ~fav; response by feature_008 within unfav
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup N=34319. Response: feature_008=1 -> 0.158, feature_008=0 -> 0.151, delta = +0.007 (logistic OR ~ 1.05, p > 0.05). Confirms h22 -- treatment is futile in these patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_002 (continuous, 1.5-5.5) has a small positive main effect on objective_response that survives multivariable adjustment.
feature_020 (continuous, 0-24, mean 3.8) has a negative main effect on objective_response that survives multivariable adjustment.
feature_024 (continuous, 0.05-300) has a small negative main effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: univariate point-biserial; multivariable logit coefficient for feature_002
analyses.result_summary: Univariate: mean feature_002 = 3.820 in responders vs 3.796 in non (delta +0.024, p ~ 4e-6). Multivariable beta = +0.127 (p ~ 2e-8). Supports h23.

analyses.code: univariate point-biserial; multivariable logit coefficient for feature_020
analyses.result_summary: Univariate mean feature_020 = 3.50 (responders) vs 3.95 (non), delta -0.454, p ~ 3e-29. Multivariable beta = -0.036 per unit, p ~ 2e-33. Supports h24.

analyses.code: univariate point-biserial; multivariable logit coefficient for feature_024
analyses.result_summary: Univariate delta = -0.533 (mean 5.69 vs 6.23), p ~ 5e-8. Multivariable beta = -0.007 per unit, p ~ 9e-8. Supports h25.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 is highly skewed (median 16, max 3622) -- a log transformation produces a smoother dose-response: log(feature_022) has a stronger univariate association with non-response than raw feature_022.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ log1p(feature_022)); compare AIC vs raw feature_022 model
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logit on log1p(feature_022): beta = -0.526, p ~ 0. Pseudo-R2 = 0.044 vs 0.013 for raw feature_022 -- log transform captures the non-linearity better. Supports h26 and reinforces that feature_022 is the dominant prognostic biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_010 (range 6-10, plausibly Gleason score) has no main effect on objective_response and no interaction with the candidate treatment feature_008.

analyses.code: groupby(feature_010).objective_response.mean(); logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 * feature_010)
analyses.result_summary: Response rate by feature_010 level (6/7/8/9/10): all between 0.235 and 0.245. Spearman r = 0.002 (p=0.61). Treatment-by-feature_010 interaction p = 0.59. Supports h27 -- feature_010 is not a meaningful predictor or modifier in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 (likely age, 30-90) has no detectable effect on objective_response and does not modify the feature_008 treatment effect.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_016); logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 * z(feature_016))
analyses.result_summary: Univariate point-biserial r = +0.001 (p=0.82). Treatment-x-feature_016 interaction p = 0.92. Supports h28: age (or whatever 30-90 attribute it represents) is not prognostic or predictive in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Lab-like continuous features feature_007 (128-152, plausibly sodium), feature_026 (6-18, plausibly hemoglobin), feature_032 (7.3-11.8, plausibly calcium), feature_014 (2.5-6.3, plausibly potassium), feature_028 (3-36, plausibly BUN), feature_018 (48-830, plausibly alkaline phosphatase) are not main-effect predictors of objective_response in this cohort.

analyses.code: pointbiserialr for each

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous renal/lab features feature_009, feature_029, feature_003, feature_012, feature_025, feature_031 are not main-effect predictors of objective_response.

analyses.code: pointbiserialr for each
analyses.result_summary: feature_009 p=0.34, feature_029 p=0.22, feature_003 p=0.51, feature_012 p=0.18, feature_025 p=0.72, feature_031 p=0.61. None < 0.10. Supports h30.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Holding feature_008=0 (no treatment) constant, the prognostic predictors that drive baseline response are (negatively) feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027, feature_001, feature_022, feature_020, feature_024 and (positively) feature_002, with feature_008 contributing zero by definition.

analyses.code: untreated = df[df.feature_008==0]; sm.Logit(untreated.objective_response, sm.add_constant(untreated[features_except_008]))
analyses.result_summary: Among untreated patients (N=29906), the same set of variables emerges as significant: feature_013 beta=-0.13 (p~0.001), feature_015 beta~0 (p~0.4) -- attenuated, feature_021 beta=-0.13, feature_027 beta=-0.05, feature_001 beta=-0.39 (p~0), feature_022 beta=-0.001 (p~0), feature_020 beta=-0.040 (p~0), feature_002 beta=+0.16 (p~0.001). Confirms a baseline prognostic model independent of treatment. Supports h31.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tree-based subgroup discovery (decision tree for objective_response with all features) recovers the same dominant split structure: feature_008 at the top among feature_013=0 patients.

analyses.code: DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=4) on all features predicting objective_response; inspect splits
analyses.result_summary: Tree splits at depth 1 on feature_013 (gini-optimal). Within feature_013=0, next split is feature_008. Within feature_013=0, feature_008=1, next splits use feature_015 and feature_021. The top three leaves correspond exactly to the 'fully favorable AND treated' subgroup (h19) with predicted response ~0.80. Supports h32; corroborates the hand-built joint subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A simple risk-stratified rule -- 'predicted responder = feature_008=1 AND feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0' -- has positive predictive value for objective_response above 75% in this cohort.

analyses.code: rule = (feature_008==1)&(feature_013==0)&(feature_015==0)&(feature_021==0)&(feature_027==0); confusion vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Rule fires on N=6406 patients. Among those, response rate (PPV) = 0.798 vs 0.157 in everyone else (negative predictive value 0.843). Sensitivity 0.426; specificity 0.966. Supports h33 -- the rule has very high PPV.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no second independent treatment in the dataset -- no other binary feature exhibits a meaningful, treatment-by-marker HTE pattern after Bonferroni adjustment.

analyses.code: exhaustive search: for each candidate treatment in [feature_005, 006, 011, 017, 019, 023, 004], compute interaction p with each of the other 31 features; threshold at 0.05/31 = 0.0016
analyses.result_summary: Across 7 candidate treatments x 31 features = 217 interaction tests, no test achieves p < 0.0016. Smallest p-values were 0.010 (feature_006 x feature_019, feature_019 x feature_006). Supports h34 -- only feature_008 functions as a treatment with HTE.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for the four binary effect modifiers (feature_013/015/021/027), the continuous feature_022 does NOT add further heterogeneity to the feature_008 treatment effect (i.e. the four binary markers fully capture the modification axis encoded by feature_022).

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 * (feature_013 + feature_015 + feature_021 + feature_027 + log1p(feature_022)))
analyses.result_summary: When all four binary effect modifiers and log1p(feature_022) are entered with treatment interactions simultaneously, the four binary feature_008-x-marker interactions remain highly significant (p<1e-9 each). The feature_008 x log1p(feature_022) interaction shrinks to beta=-0.18, p~0.005 -- not negligible but markedly attenuated. Partially supports h35: feature_022 adds a small residual modifier signal beyond the four binaries, consistent with the binaries being approximate (but not perfect) summaries of the underlying biomarker axis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final summary subgroup hypothesis: feature_008 (treatment) raises objective_response strongly and almost exclusively in the subgroup defined by feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0 (and additionally optimized at feature_001=0). Outside this subgroup the treatment effect is clinically negligible. The four 'unfavorable' binary markers and the closely related continuous feature_022 collectively define a refractory disease state.

analyses.code: Subgroup definition exactly as stated; compare absolute treatment effect inside vs outside.
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (feature_013=0 & feature_015=0 & feature_021=0 & feature_027=0): N=15681, treatment effect of feature_008 = +0.626 absolute (0.798 vs 0.172, OR 18.98, p~0). Outside subgroup: N=34319, treatment effect = +0.007 absolute (0.158 vs 0.151, p>0.05). Difference in differences = +0.62 absolute. With additional feature_001=0 restriction (N=5511) treatment effect rises to +0.646 (0.859 vs 0.213). This is the final, best-supported HTE hypothesis for objective_response in this cohort.
